meta_title: Man and Van Eastbourne | Same-Day & Small Moves | Mark Ratcliffe
meta_description: Reliable man and van service in Eastbourne. Same-day, single items, small moves. Same BAR-trained crews, lower price point. From £55/hour.
h1: Man and Van Eastbourne — Same Trusted Team, Smaller Job
intro_paragraph: Not every move needs a 7.5-tonne lorry and a full pre-pack. For studio flats, 1-bedroom moves, single bulky items, student moves and IKEA collections, our man-and-van service in Eastbourne offers the same trained crews and pad-wrap protection at a lower hourly price point. We are still a BAR-registered firm; we just use a smaller vehicle and bill by the hour rather than as a fixed quote.

sections.body_html: Not every move needs a 7.5-tonne lorry and a full pre-pack. For studio flats, 1-bedroom moves, single bulky items, student moves and IKEA collections, our man-and-van service in Eastbourne offers the same trained crews and pad-wrap protection at a lower hourly price point. We are still a BAR-registered firm; we just use a smaller vehicle and bill by the hour rather than as a fixed quote.

sections.heading: When man-and-van is right for you
sections.body_html:
When man-and-van is right for you

  • Studio or 1-bedroom flat moves within Eastbourne or East Sussex
  • Single bulky items — a sofa, a fridge-freezer, a piano, a workshop bench
  • IKEA or furniture-shop collections — let us pick up your flatpacks while you carry on with your day
  • eBay / Facebook Marketplace runs — both ends of the country if needed
  • Student moves — between halls, into rentals, end-of-summer storage
  • Storage transfers — moving items between our storage and your home
  • Clearance light — taking unwanted items to charity or the tip

sections.heading: What is included
sections.body_html:
What is included

Driver plus 1 or 2 movers

Choose one helper or two. One helper is fine for most studio and 1-bed moves; two helpers speed up larger jobs and reduce the hours billed.

Luton van or 3.5-tonne vehicle

Our man-and-van vehicles have load areas of around 600 cubic feet — equivalent to a studio flat or a generously full 1-bedroom flat.

Blankets, straps, dollies

Standard equipment included — pad-wrap blankets, ratchet straps, sack barrows, furniture sliders. No extra equipment fees.

Optional packing materials

Need boxes too? We can drop materials in advance or you can collect them from our Lower Dicker depot.

sections.heading: Same-day service — when we can
sections.body_html:
Same-day service — when we can

Many removal firms advertise "same-day" knowing they will charge a premium when you call. We do not. We will tell you honestly whether we have a crew available today, and if we do, the price is the standard hourly rate. If not, we will offer the next available slot — usually within 24-48 hours during the off-peak season.

sections.heading: Pricing
sections.body_html:
Pricing

ConfigurationFromNotes
Driver + 1 mover, Luton van£55 / hour2-hour minimum, weekday rate
Driver + 2 movers, Luton van£75 / hour2-hour minimum, weekday rate
Driver + 2 movers, 3.5-tonne£85 / hour2-hour minimum, weekday rate
Weekend / evening / bank holiday+25%Applied to the relevant hourly rate

Prices include all standard moving equipment. Long-distance jobs (outside East Sussex) are quoted as a fixed rate rather than hourly.

sections.heading: Areas we cover
sections.body_html:
Areas we cover

Our man-and-van service covers the whole BN postcode area and surrounding districts: Eastbourne, Hailsham, Polegate, Pevensey, Willingdon, Uckfield, Heathfield, Bexhill and the surrounding Sussex coast and Weald. We will travel further for the right job — ask us.

sections.heading: How man-and-van differs from a full removal — and which is right for you
sections.body_html:
How man-and-van differs from a full removal — and which is right for you

Customers sometimes ask whether man-and-van is a "cheaper version" of a full removal. The truthful answer is no — it is a different service for a different job. A full removal is a fixed-price, planned, surveyed move with packing, dedicated lorry capacity and a structured day. Man-and-van is an hourly-billed, smaller-scale service for jobs where a surveyed quote would be overkill. For a 1-bedroom flat move within Eastbourne, man-and-van is almost always cheaper and entirely appropriate. For a 3-bedroom house move, man-and-van billed hourly typically ends up more expensive (and more stressful) than a properly quoted full removal — because the hours add up faster than people expect.

sections.heading: Man-and-van jobs we run every week
sections.body_html:
Man-and-van jobs we run every week

These are the kinds of jobs that make up the majority of our man-and-van bookings around Eastbourne — and the kinds of jobs full-service movers either decline or quote unreasonably high for.

  • Studio and bedsit moves — typically 2-3 hours with a Luton van and one helper plus driver
  • Single-item collections — a sofa from Sussex Furniture, a wardrobe from Marketplace, a fridge from John Lewis
  • Pre-purchase pickups — collecting a private-sale item before completion at your new home
  • Garage and shed clearances — taking workshop kit, bikes and tools to a new property or to charity
  • End-of-tenancy moves — small flat moves where the tenant manages most of the packing
  • Auction collections — picking up purchases from local auction houses
  • Storage transfers — moving items between your home and our Prestige rooms
  • Inter-property shifts — moving rooms of furniture between two properties you own
  • Light office moves — single-room offices, home office equipment

sections.heading: How to keep your man-and-van booking efficient
sections.body_html:
How to keep your man-and-van booking efficient

Because the service is billed hourly, the things you can do before the crew arrives translate directly into pounds saved:

  • Be packed before the van arrives. Everything in boxes, taped shut, stacked near the front door. The crew should not be waiting for you to pack.
  • Pre-dismantle awkward furniture. Beds, flat-pack wardrobes and large IKEA pieces are faster if you have already dismantled them. Keep the fixings in labelled bags.
  • Park-clear at both ends. If parking right outside is uncertain, move your own car earlier to keep the space.
  • Have a destination plan. Know which room each item is going to. Tell the crew on arrival — saves "where do you want this?" trips.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
sections.body_html:
Frequently Asked Questions

How much is your man and van service in Eastbourne?

Our hourly rate starts from £55 per hour for one mover plus driver, with a 2-hour minimum. A weekend or evening surcharge applies. Larger vans and additional movers are quoted separately.

Can you do same-day man and van in Eastbourne?

Sometimes — it depends on our diary. We do not promise same-day in advance; call us and we will tell you honestly what we can fit in today.

What is the difference between man-and-van and a full removal?

Man-and-van is hourly, uses a smaller vehicle (Luton or 3.5-tonne), suits small flats, single items and student moves. A full removal is quoted as a fixed job, uses larger lorries and includes our full pad-wrap protection and packing service.

Do you supply boxes for man-and-van jobs?

Yes — we can drop materials in advance or you can collect from our Lower Dicker depot. Pad-wrap and blankets are included with the vehicle.

Can I use man-and-van for an IKEA collection?

Absolutely — IKEA flat-pack collections, eBay pickups and Facebook Marketplace runs are some of our most common man-and-van jobs. Tell us the address and dimensions and we will quote.

sections.heading: Related pages on our site
sections.body_html:
Related pages on our site

  • Moving House Checklist for Eastbourne Movers — 8-Week Plan
  • How Much Do Removals Cost in Eastbourne? 2026 Price Guide

From £55/hour · BAR-trained crews · Pad-wrap included

Man and Van Eastbourne — Same Trusted Team, Smaller Job

Get a Free Quote Call 01323 848 008
